differences.title: 1. PRIMARY FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Optimized for speed with server-side rendering and minimal JavaScript.
differences.rightDesc: Versatile framework for static and dynamic applications, server-rendered React.

differences.title: 2. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Renders components with hydration on demand, reducing JS payload.
differences.rightDesc: Offers static site generation, server-side rendering, incremental static regeneration.

differences.title: 3. JS BY DEFAULT
differences.leftDesc: Ship zero JavaScript to the client by default for faster loads.
differences.rightDesc: Ships JavaScript for client-side interactivity and React's hydration.

differences.title: 4. FRAMEWORK AGNOSTIC
differences.leftDesc: Use multiple UI frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte) in one project.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily built around the React ecosystem.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Aggressively optimizes for perceived load speed and Lighthouse scores.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent performance, but can have larger bundles than Astro.

differences.title: 6. API ROUTES
differences.leftDesc: Does not have integrated API routes like Next.js.
differences.rightDesc: Provides built-in API routes for backend functionality.

differences.title: 7. COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Easier to grasp for content-centric sites and static generation.
differences.rightDesc: More features mean a steeper learning curve for advanced use cases.
